title: Site Hierarchy Inventory
dataSources: index=catalyst, sourcetype cisco:dnac:site:topology (GET /dna/intent/api/v1/topology/site-topology; fields siteId, siteType, siteName, parentSiteName)
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:site:topology" | stats dc(siteId) as total_sites values(siteType) as types by parentSiteName | sort parentSiteName
description: Provides a complete inventory of the Catalyst Center site hierarchy (areas, buildings, floors) for infrastructure documentation and capacity planning.
value: Understanding the site hierarchy is foundational for location-based analytics. This inventory ensures Splunk has a complete map of the physical infrastructure.
implementation: Enable the `site_topology` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA pointing to `index=catalyst`. The TA calls `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/topology/site-topology` on a **3600s** (1 hour) default interval. Key fields: `siteId`, `siteType`, `siteName`, `parentSiteName`.
visualization: Hierarchical or indented table (parentSiteName, total_sites, types), tree or Sankey if exported to a viz app, single value of global site count in a subsearch if needed.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538); **site topology** in `index=catalyst`, sourcetype `cisco:dnac:site:topology`.
‚Ä¢ **Catalyst** **Design** / **NOC** owner who **maintains** the **hierarchy** in **Catalyst**‚ÄîSplunk is a **read-only** **mirror**; **mismatches** are fixed in **Catalyst** first.
‚Ä¢ **Process:** when you **onboard** new **buildings** or **renovations**, **refresh** the **topology** in **Catalyst** and **re-validate** this **search** the **same** **week**.
‚Ä¢ `docs/implementation-guide.md` for **TA** **config** and **index** **routing**.

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
‚Ä¢ **Intent API:** `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/topology/site-topology` (site **hierarchy**; **Catalyst** **version** may **namespace** the path‚Äîverify **in** the **API** **browser** of **your** **release**).
‚Ä¢ **TA input name:** **site_topology**; sourcetype `cisco:dnac:site:topology`.
‚Ä¢ **Default interval:** **3600 seconds (1 hour)**‚Äî**structure** **changes** are **rare**; do **not** over-poll **without** **reason**.
‚Ä¢ **Key fields:** `siteId`, `siteType` (for example **area** / **building** / **floor**‚Äîvalues **depend** on **your** **design**), `siteName`, `parentSiteName`.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and report
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:site:topology" | stats dc(siteId) as total_sites values(siteType) as types by parentSiteName | sort parentSiteName
```

Understanding this SPL
‚Ä¢ **`parentSiteName` as the by-clause** gives a **parent** **rollup**; if **Catalyst** **nests** **multiple** **levels** **under** a **regional** **root**, you may need a **separate** **panel** **by** **`siteName`** for **leaves** only.
‚Ä¢ **`values(siteType)`** is **for** **sanity** **checking**‚Äîit is **not** a **compliance** **count**; **one** **parent** can **host** many **child** **types**.

**Pipeline walkthrough**
‚Ä¢ Scopes the **site** **feed**; **dedupes** **IDs** per **parent**; **orders** for **NOC** **naming** **comfort** with **Catalyst** **UI** **navigation**.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
‚Ä¢ **Open** **Catalyst** **>** **Network** **>** **Sites** and **compare** the **set** of **`siteName`** and **`parentSiteName`** to **rows** in **Splunk** for the **last** **poll**‚Äîallow **relabel** **lag** of **up** to **one** **hour**.
‚Ä¢ If **`total_sites` == 0** for a **parent** you **expect**, **re-run** a **broad** **search** **`| stats count by siteName`** to **see** if **Catalyst** **moved** **sites** **under** a **new** **regional** **parent**.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
‚Ä¢ **Dashboard:** **indented** **table** of **rollups**; **export** a **CSV** to **Confluence** or the **CMDB** for **on-call** **runbooks** that **name** **sites** the **same** way as **Catalyst**.
‚Ä¢ **Join** with **other** **UCs** (for example **device** or **AP** by **siteId** **when** **available**) using a **lookup** table **keyed** by **`siteId`**‚Äî**rebuild** the **lookup** on a **nightly** **schedule** when **topology** **changes** are **frequent**.

Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshooting
‚Ä¢ **Empty** **index:** **site_topology** **input** **disabled** or **wrong** **Catalyst** **URL**‚Äî**check** **TA** **logs**.
‚Ä¢ **Duplicate** **`siteId`:** **TA** or **Catalyst** **re-emitted** a **reparent**; **use** `| dedup siteId` before **stats** if **necessary**.
‚Ä¢ **Extra** **parents** in **Splunk** not in **Catalyst**:** **old** **cache**; **bump** **TA** **or** **clear** the **input** **checkpoint** per **Cisco** **doc** (careful in **prod**‚Äîcoordinate).
‚Ä¢ **Orphaned** **sites** after **mergers:** a **Catalyst** **admin** must **reassign** **parents**; **Splunk** will **not** ‚Äúfix‚Äù **governance** **errors** in **Catalyst** itself.
grandmaExplanation: We map out your entire network site structure ‚Äî areas, buildings, and floors ‚Äî so every device and problem can be tied to a physical location.
